title: Reading Legend — how to parse every diagram in this book
summary: A pedagogical key — the one diagram in this book that isn't a real causal system. Every visual convention the reader will encounter 62 times is shown exactly once: the five node categories (driver, mediator, outcome, feedback, latent), the two edge polarities (+ solid green, − solid red), delay marks (double ticks on the arrow path), confounding arcs (dashed, from a latent node), and the two loop types (R reinforcing when negative edges are even, B balancing when odd). Read this figure once, then every other figure in the book will be legible.
source: Preface §How to Read This Book — the visual vocabulary used across all 62 figures

nodes.label: Driver Node
nodes.description: An input variable. Starts a causal chain. Rendered dark blue.

nodes.label: Mediator Node
nodes.description: A pass-through variable. Sits in the middle of a causal chain. Rendered purple.

nodes.label: Outcome Node
nodes.description: The result of the system. Rendered orange. Gets the identifiability badge (✓ or ✗).

nodes.label: Feedback Node
nodes.description: A homeostatic regulator. Closes a loop by correcting deviations. Rendered dark green.

nodes.label: Latent Node
nodes.description: An unobserved variable. Dashed gray ellipse. Hidden in CLD mode, visible in DAG mode.

edges.description: Positive edge: 'when A rises, B rises'. Solid green line with a + badge.
Another positive edge, closing the forward chain driver → mediator → outcome.
Positive edge with a delay (double-tick marks on the arrow path).
Closes the reinforcing loop R1 back to the driver.
Negative edge: 'when A rises, B falls'. Solid red line with a − badge.
Confounding arc from a latent node. Dashed gray, shown only in DAG or Both mode.
Another confounding arc — latent nodes usually confound multiple downstream nodes.

loops.label: R1 — example reinforcing loop (0 negative edges)
B1 — example balancing loop (1 negative edge)

identifiability_note: This is a pedagogical legend, not a real causal system. Its 'identifiability' is that all five node categories, both edge polarities, delay marks, confounding arcs, and both loop types are demonstrated once in a single figure. Read this legend first; every other figure in the book uses the same conventions.
